**Acceptance Criteria**

Fail. The prior `commands/template.rs` duplication has been addressed: it now calls `label_utils::type_label_value` and `label_utils::is_type_label`, for example [template.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/template.rs:202) and [template.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/template.rs:945).

Blocking issue: the “single shared helper is the only place that knows the `type:` label encoding” criterion is still not met outside the originally named command sites:

- [type_hierarchy.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/type_hierarchy.rs:243) still documents and implements its own `"type:value"` extractor. [type_hierarchy.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/type_hierarchy.rs:249) hard-codes `parts[0] != "type"` instead of using the shared `TYPE_NAMESPACE` / `type_label_value`.
- [domain/projection.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/domain/projection.rs:144) defines a second private `TYPE_NAMESPACE`, then uses it to select the projected issue type at [domain/projection.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/domain/projection.rs:197).
- [validation/graph.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/validation/graph.rs:889) still calls `values_in_namespace(issue, "type")` for type-label semantics.

The exact raw `starts_with("type:")` / `strip_prefix("type:")` patterns are gone, and the five issue-listed command sites do call the shared helper, e.g. [issue.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:36), [batch_create.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/batch_create.rs:553), [breakdown.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/breakdown.rs:607), and [validate.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:838). But the broader single-owner criterion still fails.

**Architecture / Separation**

No storage, command, CLI, or output boundary regression found in the reviewed label-helper changes. The remaining issue is domain/validation code retaining parallel type-label knowledge instead of depending on the shared label primitive.

**Functional Paradigm**

No new imperative debt found in the reviewed helper usage. The new helper in [labels.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/worktrees/agent-e3e6d47d/crates/jit/src/labels.rs:190) is pure and iterator-based.

**Correctness / Invariants**

No new issue found for atomic writes, event logging, gate semantics, assignee format, label format validation, PID safety, or unsafe code. `#![deny(unsafe_code)]` remains present in the workspace entry points.

The centralization invariant for type-label parsing is the blocker.
